executiveSummary: The ADE - Trade semantic model scores 38/100 on overall health, driven by critical performance bottlenecks in its DirectQuery architecture. The model has 45 tables (9 DirectQuery, 29 Dual, 7 Import), 801 DAX measures, and 31 relationships (3 bidirectional). The central fact table — fact_product_option_trade_daily_snapshot_v1 (116 GB, 91 columns) — receives 585,000 non-cached queries per month from PBI, averaging 4.6 seconds each with a P95 of 15.3 seconds. The #1 root cause is the date-filter-via-JOIN pattern: PBI's generated SQL joins dim_date with a WHERE on calendar_date, then joins the fact table via surrogate key. Because Databricks cannot push the date predicate through the JOIN, cluster pruning on dim_date_sk is nearly useless (0.18% pruning efficiency), causing full scans of 5.6 billion rows and 115+ GB per query. The single most impactful recommendation is to create a narrow, pre-filtered aggregation table in Databricks that the model can use as a Hybrid (Import/DQ) source, reducing query data by 90%+.

modelDesign.notes: 3 bidirectional relationships, 1 M:M, 4 wide tables (>50 cols), 8 Dual tables that could be Import. 650 unused column candidates.

daxQuality.notes: 77 FILTER(ALL) anti-patterns generating full scans in DQ mode. 55 measures with 5+ estimated SQL subqueries. 54 nested CALCULATE patterns.

queryPerformance.notes: Avg 4.6s, P95 15.3s for fact table queries. Slowest queries reach 500s (Option Ranking) and 225s (standard DQ). Near-zero file pruning on typical queries (0.18%).

architecture.notes: All serve views are pass-through SELECT *. Liquid clustering exists on fact table but is ineffective due to date-filter-via-JOIN pattern. No aggregation tables. Engineering BPA: 6 high findings.

databricksDailyStats.note: Stats cover ALL PBI SPN queries (all models), not just Trade. Trade fact table alone accounts for 585K non-cached queries.

topFindings.title: Date Filter via JOIN Prevents Cluster Pruning on Fact Table
topFindings.description: PBI's DirectQuery engine generates SQL that applies date filters to dim_date_v2 (WHERE calendar_date >= '2024-04-13' AND calendar_date < '2026-04-13'), then JOINs to the fact table via dim_date_sk (surrogate key). Because Databricks cannot push the date predicate through the JOIN, the fact table's liquid clustering on dim_date_sk is nearly useless — only 5 of 2,736 files are pruned (0.18% efficiency). This forces full scans of the entire 116 GB fact table on every query.
topFindings.recommendation: Create a narrow, date-filtered aggregation table in dbt that pre-aggregates the most common Trade metrics by date, product option, and the key dimension combinations. Use Incremental Refresh or a Composite Model in PBI so that historical data is imported and only the current period uses DirectQuery. Alternatively, add a direct date column (calendar_date) to the fact table and create a PBI M-level parameter that pushes the date filter as a WHERE clause directly on the fact table, enabling cluster pruning.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 90%+ reduction in rows read per query. P95 from 15s to ~1-2s for typical visuals.

connectionModeComparison.directQuery: Current — avg 4.6s, P95 15.3s, full scans of 116 GB
connectionModeComparison.hybrid: Aggregation table serves 80%+ of visuals in <1s, DQ fallback for detail
connectionModeComparison.import: Sub-second for all visuals. Requires ~116 GB import — likely exceeds model size limits
connectionModeComparison.parameterFilter: M-level date parameter pushes filter to fact table, enabling cluster pruning. Reduces scan to ~10% of table.

topFindings.dependencyNote: Maximum benefit requires narrow serve views (F3) to reduce column selection alongside date pruning.
tradeoffs: Aggregation table requires ongoing dbt maintenance and compute for materialisation
Incremental Refresh setup requires Premium/Fabric capacity configuration
M-level parameter filter changes the user experience (date slicer becomes a parameter)

options.name: Option A: Aggregation Table (Recommended)
options.description: Create a dbt model that pre-aggregates Trade fact data by day × product option × pricing store into a Delta table. PBI uses this as an aggregation source.
options.pros: Dramatic reduction in query volume and latency. Works with existing PBI architecture.
options.cons: Requires new dbt model and PBI aggregation table configuration. Ongoing compute cost.

options.name: Option B: M-Level Date Parameter Filter
options.description: Add a calendar_date column to the serve view and use PBI's native filtering capabilities to push the date predicate directly to the fact table.
options.pros: No new tables needed. Enables cluster pruning immediately.
options.cons: Requires serve view and PBI expression changes. May not cover all date filter scenarios.

options.name: Option C: Composite Model (Import + DQ)
options.description: Import historical data (>90 days) and use DirectQuery only for current period.
options.pros: Historical queries become sub-second. Only current period hits Databricks.
options.cons: Requires Premium capacity. Import refresh schedule needed. Model size increases.

topFindings.title: 77 FILTER(ALL) Anti-Patterns Generate Full Table Scans in DirectQuery
topFindings.description: 77 DAX measures use the FILTER(ALL(Table)) pattern, which in DirectQuery mode forces a separate SQL subquery that scans the entire table with no filter context. Combined with 55 measures generating 5+ SQL subqueries each, a single visual displaying multiple measures can trigger dozens of full table scans against the 116 GB fact table.
topFindings.recommendation: Refactor the highest-impact FILTER(ALL) measures using REMOVEFILTERS() or ALLEXCEPT() patterns. Prioritise measures referenced in the most-used report pages. For WTD/MTD/YTD time intelligence measures that use FILTER(ALL('Date')), consider pre-calculating these periods in dbt and storing as flags on the fact table, eliminating the DAX context transition entirely.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 30-50% reduction in SQL subquery count per visual. Eliminates forced full-scan subqueries for refactored measures.
suggestedAnalysisSteps: Export the 77 FILTER(ALL) measures from dax-audit.json
Cross-reference with report pages that have the most visuals (Trade has 12 pages with 274 visuals)
Run top 10 measures in DAX Studio with Server Timings to see generated SQL
Identify which FILTER(ALL) patterns can be replaced with REMOVEFILTERS()
For time intelligence measures (WTD, MTD, YTD), consider dbt pre-calculation approach
tradeoffs: DAX refactoring requires testing each measure for correct results
Some FILTER(ALL) usages are intentional (e.g., percentage-of-total calculations) and cannot be trivially replaced
Effort is proportional to measure count — 77 measures is significant

topFindings.title: Serve View Exposes 91 Columns — PBI Over-Selects All of Them
topFindings.description: The serve view for fact_product_option_trade_daily_snapshot_v1 is a pass-through SELECT * that exposes all 91 columns of the curated table. PBI's DirectQuery engine generates SQL selecting ALL 91 columns even when a visual only uses a few aggregated values. While Delta's columnar storage mitigates some of the I/O impact, the wide projection increases memory pressure and network transfer.
topFindings.recommendation: Create a narrow serve view (serve_fact_trade_daily_narrow_v1) in dbt that selects only the ~25 columns actually referenced by PBI measures and relationships. This requires analysing which columns are used in DAX measures, relationship keys, and visual bindings. The existing wide view can remain for backward compatibility.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 20-40% reduction in read_bytes per query. Reduces memory pressure and network transfer.
tradeoffs: Requires analysis of which columns PBI actually uses
May need periodic updates if new measures reference additional columns
Two serve views to maintain (narrow + wide for backward compatibility)

topFindings.title: Option Ranking Query Scans Fact Table 12 Times (500s)
topFindings.description: The Option Ranking table is populated by a native SQL query (not standard PBI DirectQuery) that uses a CTE with 12 UNION ALL branches. Each branch performs a separate GROUP BY and ROW_NUMBER() on the fact table filtered to yesterday's date. While each individual branch benefits from clustering (95% pruning), the cumulative effect of 12 sequential scans results in a 500-second query. This is the single slowest query in the Trade model.
topFindings.recommendation: Replace the 12-branch CTE with a single-pass unpivot approach: scan the fact table once, compute all 6 metrics in a single GROUP BY, then use LATERAL VIEW or UNPIVOT to create the ranking rows. Alternatively, materialise the ranking as a scheduled dbt model that runs daily, reducing the query to a simple table read.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: Query time from 500s to ~40-50s (single-pass) or <1s (materialised table).

options.name: Option A: Single-Pass SQL Refactor
options.description: Rewrite the CTE to compute all metrics in one GROUP BY, then unpivot into ranking rows.
options.pros: No new infrastructure. 10-12x reduction in scan volume.
options.cons: Requires modifying the PBI expression or dbt model that generates this query.

options.name: Option B: Materialised Ranking Table (Recommended)
options.description: Create a dbt model that materialises the ranking table daily as a Delta table.
options.pros: Sub-second query from PBI. Eliminates all 12 scans. Can be Import mode.
options.cons: Requires new dbt model and scheduling. Daily refresh dependency.

tradeoffs: Materialised approach adds dbt pipeline dependency
Option Ranking is already a separate Databricks table — may be from a custom dbt model (dbt.rafael_diassantos.option_ranking)

topFindings.title: 3 Bidirectional Cross-Filter Relationships on DirectQuery Tables
topFindings.description: The model has 3 bidirectional cross-filter relationships. In DirectQuery mode, bidirectional filtering requires the engine to generate additional SQL subqueries for the reverse filter direction, potentially doubling query complexity for visuals that traverse these relationships.
topFindings.recommendation: Review each bidirectional relationship to determine if it is genuinely needed. Replace with CROSSFILTER() in specific measures where bidirectional filtering is only needed conditionally. If a relationship is bidirectional solely to enable a single visual, consider using TREATAS() or a bridge table instead.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 10-20% reduction in SQL subquery count for visuals traversing these relationships.
suggestedAnalysisSteps: Identify the 3 bidirectional relationships from model-taxonomy.json
For each, determine which visuals require the reverse filter direction
Test removing bidirectional and using CROSSFILTER() in specific measures
tradeoffs: Removing bidirectional may break existing visuals that depend on reverse filtering
CROSSFILTER() adds DAX complexity

topFindings.title: 650 Unused Column Candidates Increase Model Complexity
topFindings.description: BPA identifies 650 columns that are not referenced by any DAX measure, relationship, or hierarchy. While hidden columns may still be used by report-level filters or visual bindings, this is a significant number of potentially unused columns that add to model complexity and metadata size.
topFindings.recommendation: Export the 650 unused column candidates. Cross-reference with PBIX visual bindings from visual-analysis.json. Hide or remove columns that are confirmed unused. For DirectQuery tables, removing columns from the model (not the source) reduces the column projection in generated SQL.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: Reduced model metadata size. Potentially fewer columns in generated SQL queries.
tradeoffs: Columns may be used by report-level filters not detected by BPA
Users may rely on hidden columns for ad-hoc analysis in connected Excel

topFindings.title: Auto-Refresh Enabled on Trade Report — Continuous Background Queries
topFindings.description: The Trade report has auto-refresh enabled, which triggers periodic re-queries of all visible visuals against Databricks. With 274 visuals across 12 pages and a DirectQuery fact table, this generates a continuous stream of expensive queries even when no user is actively viewing the report.
topFindings.recommendation: Disable auto-refresh on the Trade report. If near-real-time data is needed, limit auto-refresh to a single dedicated page with minimal visuals, not the entire report.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: Eliminates background query load. Could reduce total Trade queries by 10-30% depending on refresh interval.
tradeoffs: Users who leave the report open may see stale data until they manually refresh
If business requires near-real-time trade monitoring, a dedicated lightweight page is the alternative

topFindings.title: 12 Pages with Too Many Visuals (Trade Report Has 274 Visuals)
topFindings.description: The Trade report has 274 visuals across 12 pages, with multiple pages exceeding recommended visual counts. Each visible visual triggers a separate DirectQuery request. Pages with 20+ visuals generate 20+ concurrent queries against Databricks, creating contention and increasing page load times.
topFindings.recommendation: Reduce visual count per page to <15. Move detail tables to drill-through pages. Use bookmarks to show/hide visual groups rather than displaying all at once. Consider tabbed pages to split dense layouts.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 30-50% reduction in concurrent queries per page load. Faster page render times.
tradeoffs: Users may need to navigate more to find information
Requires report redesign and user acceptance

topFindings.title: 19 Wide Table/Matrix Visuals Generate Excessive Column Requests
topFindings.description: The Trade report contains 19 wide table or matrix visuals. Each additional column in a table visual adds to the SQL column selection in DirectQuery mode. Wide tables with 10+ columns generate particularly expensive queries.
topFindings.recommendation: Limit table visuals to essential columns. Move detailed breakdowns to drill-through pages or export-to-Excel scenarios. Use conditional columns or tooltips to provide detail without adding visual columns.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: 10-20% reduction in query width for affected visuals.
tradeoffs: Users may want all columns visible for quick scanning

topFindings.title: Visual Fetching 1M+ Rows from DirectQuery
topFindings.description: At least one visual fetches 1,000,001 rows from the fact table, reading 6.2 billion rows and 16.5 GB in the process, with 37 seconds spent just fetching the result set. This is likely a detail table or matrix showing individual product option rows without sufficient aggregation or filtering.
topFindings.recommendation: Identify the visual generating this query (likely a detail table on a ranking or drill-through page). Add Top N filtering, aggregation, or require a slicer selection before the visual loads. Consider using TOPN() in a DAX measure to limit the result set.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: Eliminates the single most data-heavy visual. Saves 37s of result fetch time alone.
tradeoffs: Users may want to see all rows — provide an export option instead

topFindings.title: 8 Dual Mode Tables Could Be Switched to Import
topFindings.description: 8 tables are in Dual mode (both Import and DirectQuery) but are small dimension tables that could safely be Import-only. Dual mode adds complexity to the engine's query plan selection and can result in unexpected DirectQuery fallback.
topFindings.recommendation: Switch small, static dimension tables from Dual to Import mode. Tables like Country, Territory, Time Period, and financial calendar tables are ideal candidates. Keep only tables that genuinely need to participate in both Import and DQ query plans in Dual mode.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: Eliminates DQ fallback for these tables. Marginal improvement in query plan simplicity.
tradeoffs: Import tables require a refresh schedule
If any DQ relationship path goes through these tables, switching to Import may break the path

topFindings.title: Missing Date Slicer on 3 Data-Heavy Pages
topFindings.description: 3 data-heavy pages in the Trade report lack a visible date slicer. Without a date slicer, users cannot narrow the date range, forcing all visuals to query the full 2-year window of data (the default PBI date filter range).
topFindings.recommendation: Add a prominent date slicer on all data-heavy pages. Set a default value to the current financial period to reduce initial page load.
topFindings.estimatedImprovement: Users can narrow date range, reducing scanned data by 50-90% for typical daily/weekly views.
tradeoffs: Takes visual real estate on the page

actions.action: Identify and fix the 1M-row visual (add Top N or require slicer selection)
Add date slicers to 3 data-heavy pages with sensible defaults
Reduce wide table visuals from 19 to essential columns only
Switch 8 small Dual dimension tables to Import mode
Hide or remove confirmed unused columns (start with 650 candidates)
Create narrow serve view (serve_fact_trade_daily_narrow_v1) with only PBI-referenced columns
Materialise Option Ranking as a daily dbt model instead of 12-branch CTE
Reduce visual density: target <15 visuals per page across Trade report
Create date-filtered aggregation table in dbt for common Trade metrics
Configure PBI aggregation table or Composite Model using the new agg table
Review 3 bidirectional relationships — replace with CROSSFILTER() where possible
Refactor top 20 highest-impact FILTER(ALL) measures using REMOVEFILTERS()
For WTD/MTD/YTD measures, evaluate dbt pre-calculation approach
